text.title: Paid Trial Task Library Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Paid Trial Task Library methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every trial task in the library MUST carry a non-zero payout; unpaid trial tasks are forbidden.
rule.rationale: unpaid trial work is exploitative and biases applicant pool against people without runway.

rule.statement: Each task MUST list an explicit time-box (e.g. 4h, 8h); over-running tasks invalidate the rubric and burn the candidate.
rule.rationale: open-ended tasks measure stamina, not skill, and select for desperation.

rule.statement: Each task MUST ship with a 3-5-criterion rubric where each criterion has anchored examples (what 'good' / 'acceptable' / 'fail' look like).
rule.rationale: without anchored rubrics, scores drift to vibes and bias creeps in.

rule.statement: Deliverable MUST be tangible (PR / doc / spec / design) that the reviewer can read; 'do a discovery call and write notes' is rejected.
rule.rationale: intangible deliverables make rubric scoring impossible.

rule.statement: Trial tasks MUST be either on synthetic data OR on scoped slices that can be safely shared with non-employees; tasks requiring full repo access are rejected.
rule.rationale: candidates are non-employees and unbounded repo access is an unjustified risk.

rule.statement: Scoring SHOULD be blinded to candidate identity where the task format permits.
rule.rationale: blinding reduces affinity bias in evaluation.
